blockers: Value completion still loses alternatives when the same property is declared in multiple parent composition branches: resolve_subschema returns only the first matching branch, so root anyOf branches defining mode as enum ["a"] and enum ["b"] yield only "a".
collect_values_from unions suggestions from allOf branches, but allOf represents intersection; overlapping enums such as ["a", "b"] and ["b", "c"] incorrectly suggest invalid values "a" and "c".

Boolean deduplication drops valid completions on partial overlap: if an earlier enum or const supplies true and a later branch has type boolean, false is inserted into seen but no suggestion containing false is emitted.
overall_reasoning: The implementation is well scoped and professionally tested, but its core value-completion algorithm treats every composition keyword as a simple union after resolving the requested property to a single schema. That approach cannot correctly represent parent-level alternatives and is semantically wrong for allOf. The Boolean aggregate variant further makes cross-kind dedup lossy. These are user-visible completion correctness defects directly within the requested feature, so the patch requires revision before merge despite its otherwise strong structure and coverage.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: COMPOSITION_KEYWORDS, schema_type_of, and collect_values_from make the intent easy to follow. However, representing Boolean as one aggregate suggestion while deduplicating individual true/false keys creates an evident partial-overlap correctness flaw, and the uniform recursion obscures the different semantics of allOf versus anyOf/oneOf.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: The implementation uses existing schema helpers, recursion limits, data types, fixture style, and LSP test infrastructure, and formatting/clippy checks were performed. Repository fit is reduced because resolve_subschema's first-match API is not sufficient for value collection across alternatives, yet it was reused rather than introducing traversal capable of returning all applicable schemas.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: The patch stays within completion schema traversal and its tests/fixture. The shared resolve_subschema change also alters hover resolution for anyOf and oneOf schemas, but this is adjacent to the requested composition support rather than broad unrelated work.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: No unsafe operations, network behavior, privilege changes, or unbounded traversal were introduced. Existing depth limits remain in place, and serialized dedup keys are deterministic for JSON values.
rating_rationales.test_quality: There is extensive unit and LSP coverage for merged properties, duplicate enums, nullable strings and booleans, nested traversal, sorting, and completion detail. The suite misses critical cases: the same property defined with different values in multiple parent alternatives, allOf value intersections, and enum/const overlap with type boolean. Those omissions allow the blockers to pass.

strengths: Changes are focused on schema completion traversal, completion fixtures, and related tests.
Property names are deduplicated consistently across allOf, anyOf, and oneOf branches.
Nullable type arrays are handled for null suggestions and primary completion detail/snippet type.
Tests cover unit-level collection and realistic end-to-end LSP completion behavior.
Traversal retains a recursion-depth limit and introduces no new external I/O or unsafe code.
summary: The patch substantially improves composed-schema and nullable completion coverage, with clear helpers and strong LSP tests, but it is not ready to merge because value composition remains semantically incomplete. Parent-level alternatives with the same property are truncated to the first branch, allOf values are incorrectly unioned rather than intersected, and partial boolean overlap can silently omit false or true.
